tspan.data-narration: This diagram illustrates the design of a decimal adder in Verilog HDL. The decimal adder is a digital circuit that performs binary addition on two decimal numbers. The process of decimal addition can be broken down into the following steps: Convert the decimal numbers to binary, Perform binary addition, and Convert the result back to decimal. To design a 5-bit decimal adder, we can use the 4-bit binary decimal adder as the building block. We will create a 5-bit binary decimal adder using 4-bit binary decimal adders in parallel. Here is the Verilog code for the 4-bit binary decimal adder:
